item.title: CreativityBench: Evaluating Agent Creative Reasoning via Affordance-Based Tool Repurposing
item.description: Recent advances in large language models have led to strong performance on reasoning and environment-interaction tasks, yet their ability for creative problem-solving remains underexplored. We study this capability through the lens of cr...

item.title: Awaking Spatial Intelligence in Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation
item.description: We present JoyAI-Image, a unified multimodal foundation model for visual understanding, text-to-image generation, and instruction-guided image editing. JoyAI-Image couples a spatially enhanced Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) with...

item.title: MiniCPM-o 4.5: Towards Real-Time Full-Duplex Omni-Modal Interaction
item.description: Recent progress in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has brought AI capabilities from static offline data processing to real-time streaming interaction, yet they still remain far from human-level multimodal interaction. The key bo...

item.title: MedSkillAudit: A Domain-Specific Audit Framework for Medical Research Agent Skills
item.description: Background: Agent skills are increasingly deployed as modular, reusable capability units in AI agent systems. Medical research agent skills require safeguards beyond general-purpose evaluation, including scientific integrity, methodologi...

item.title: XL-SafetyBench: A Country-Grounded Cross-Cultural Benchmark for LLM Safety and Cultural Sensitivity
item.description: Current LLM safety benchmarks are predominantly English-centric and often rely on translation, failing to capture country-specific harms. Moreover, they rarely evaluate a model's ability to detect culturally embedded sensitivities as dis...

item.title: ResRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Negative Sample Projection Residual Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) enhances reasoning of Large Language Models (LLMs) but usually exhibits limited generation diversity due to the over-incentivization of positive rewards. Although methods like Negativ...

item.title: Turning Drift into Constraint: Robust Reasoning Alignment in Non-Stationary Environments
item.description: This paper identifies a critical yet underexplored challenge in reasoning alignment from multiple multi-modal large language models (MLLMs): In non-stationary environments, the diverse reasoning distributions of source models often evolv...

item.title: Stream-R1: Reliability-Perplexity Aware Reward Distillation for Streaming Video Generation
item.description: Distillation-based acceleration has become foundational for making autoregressive streaming video diffusion models practical, with distribution matching distillation (DMD) as the de facto choice. Existing methods, however, train the stud...

item.title: OpenSearch-VL: An Open Recipe for Frontier Multimodal Search Agents
item.description: Deep search has become a crucial capability for frontier multimodal agents, enabling models to solve complex questions through active search, evidence verification, and multi-step reasoning. Despite rapid progress, top-tier multimodal se...

item.title: D-OPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Continuously Tuning Step-Distilled Diffusion Models
item.description: The landscape of high-performance image generation models is currently shifting from the inefficient multi-step ones to the efficient few-step counterparts (e.g, Z-Image-Turbo and FLUX.2-klein). However, these models present significant...

item.title: Diffusion Model as a Generalist Segmentation Learner
item.description: Diffusion models are primarily trained for image synthesis, yet their denoising trajectories encode rich, spatially aligned visual priors. In this paper, we demonstrate that these priors can be utilized for text-conditioned semantic and...

item.title: When to Think, When to Speak: Learning Disclosure Policies for LLM Reasoning
item.description: In single-stream autoregressive interfaces, the same tokens both update the model state and constitute an irreversible public commitment. This coupling creates a silence tax: additional deliberation postpones the first task-relevant cont...

item.title: KinDER: A Physical Reasoning Benchmark for Robot Learning and Planning
item.description: Robotic systems that interact with the physical world must reason about kinematic and dynamic constraints imposed by their own embodiment, their environment, and the task at hand. We introduce KinDER, a benchmark for Kinematic and Dynami...

item.title: HERMES++: Toward a Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation
item.description: Driving world models serve as a pivotal technology for autonomous driving by simulating environmental dynamics. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on future scene generation, often overlooking comprehensive 3D scene underst...

item.title: Stream-T1: Test-Time Scaling for Streaming Video Generation
item.description: While Test-Time Scaling (TTS) offers a promising direction to enhance video generation without the surging costs of training, current test-time video generation methods based on diffusion models suffer from exorbitant candidate explorati...

item.title: RLDX-1 Technical Report
item.description: While Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) have shown remarkable progress toward human-like generalist robotic policies through the versatile intelligence (i.e. broad scene understanding and language-conditioned generalization) inherited...

item.title: PhysForge: Generating Physics-Grounded 3D Assets for Interactive Virtual World
item.description: Synthesizing physics-grounded 3D assets is a critical bottleneck for interactive virtual worlds and embodied AI. Existing methods predominantly focus on static geometry, overlooking the functional properties essential for interaction. We...

item.title: Rethinking Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval: Evaluating and Advancing Retrievers in Agentic Search Systems
item.description: Reasoning-intensive retrieval aims to surface evidence that supports downstream reasoning rather than merely matching topical similarity. This capability is increasingly important for agentic search systems, where retrievers must provide...

item.title: Lightning Unified Video Editing via In-Context Sparse Attention
item.description: Video editing has evolved toward In-Context Learning (ICL) paradigms, yet the resulting quadratic attention costs create a critical computational bottleneck. In this work, we propose In-context Sparse Attention (ISA), the first near-loss...

item.title: Parameter-Efficient Multi-View Proficiency Estimation: From Discriminative Classification to Generative Feedback
item.description: Estimating how well a person performs an action, rather than which action is performed, is central to coaching, rehabilitation, and talent identification. This task is challenging because proficiency is encoded in subtle differences in t...

item.title: SWE-WebDevBench: Evaluating Coding Agent Application Platforms as Virtual Software Agencies
item.description: The emergence of "vibe coding" platforms, where users describe applications in natural language and AI agents autonomously generate full-stack software, has created a need for rigorous evaluation beyond code-level benchmarks. In order to...

item.title: TT4D: A Pipeline and Dataset for Table Tennis 4D Reconstruction From Monocular Videos
item.description: We present TT4D, a large-scale, high-fidelity table tennis dataset. It provides 140+ hours of reconstructed singles and doubles gameplay from monocular broadcast videos, featuring multimodal annotations like high-quality camera calibrati...

item.title: A Foundation Model for Zero-Shot Logical Rule Induction
item.description: Inductive Logic Programming (ILP) learns interpretable logical rules from data. Existing methods are transductive: their learned parameters are bound to specific predicates and require retraining for each new task. We introduce Neural Ru...

item.title: APEX: Large-scale Multi-task Aesthetic-Informed Popularity Prediction for AI-Generated Music
item.description: Music popularity prediction has attracted growing research interest, with relevance to artists, platforms, and recommendation systems. However, the explosive rise of AI-generated music platforms has created an entirely new and largely un...

item.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
item.description: Self-consistency detects hallucinations by generating multiple sampled answers to a question and measuring agreement, but this requires repeated decoding and can be sensitive to lexical variation. Semantic self-consistency improves this...
